We also talked about expanding domestic drone manufacturing and bringing more shipbuilding work back to America. https://t.co/mHBBtf5GFZ

Met with Indiana’s home builders to talk housing affordability.

Builders know firsthand what drives up housing costs. Permitting delays and red tape make it harder to build. Hoosiers pay the price.

We’ve got to get Indiana building again. #IN06 https://t.co/BoXqd8Ui0E

These office hours are a chance to hear what’s on people’s minds and help with problems involving federal agencies — from VA https://t.co/pNjG8SLtNH

I visited the Richmond club today and saw what @BGCWC means for Wayne County kids — mentorship, summer activities, and a safe place to spend the day https://t.co/IeVqaztsWX

Seven high school seniors from IN-06 earned appointments to our nation’s service academies. It was a privilege to nominate each of them.

Recently, we celebrated them — and their parents —  as they prepare to report to their academies.

These appointments are super competitive. https://t.co/mWKXZPGV9I

Hayes Arboretum is one of Wayne County’s treasures.

Stanley Hayes began protecting this land in Richmond in 1915 so future generations could experience Indiana’s natural landscape. More than a century later, the Hayes family is still carrying that work forward.

I toured the https://t.co/2y5lwa625I

My thanks to our Wayne County veterans who joined my roundtable at Richmond City Hall.

Wayne County is home to more than 4,000 veterans. They’ve earned care that is timely, reliable, and close to home.

We discussed the VA system, the Richmond VA Clinic, and how Congress can https://t.co/qm9b9VxY1p

My team and I held office hours in Rushville yesterday.

These visits are one way we stay close to home and available to Hoosiers across #IN06. 

Whether it’s help with a federal agency, a question about Washington, or an issue you want us to hear, we’re here to listen and help.

I advanced $1 million through @HouseAppropsGOP this month to help fix Carthage’s sewer system. That funding is now headed to the House floor.

When heavy rain raises the Big Blue River, water pours  into the town’s sewer system. Their plant was built for a small town (pop. 900). https://t.co/7KQonjFE1m

A $200+ million investment: New jobs. Demand for 32 million additional bushels of Indiana corn each year. That’s a stronger market for Hoosier farmers, more domestic https://t.co/65JAkcBqM3

RT @HouseAppropsGOP: 11 down. 1 to go.

Today marks the 251st birthday of our United States Army.

Since 1775, the men and women of the Army have answered the call to defend this nation. We owe them — and the families who stand behind them — our gratitude.

Happy birthday to the greatest Army in the world. 🇺🇸 https://t.co/bnZJurxW32

Today we honor our American flag — and everything it stands for:

Freedom. Sacrifice. The belief that a nation built by the people can endure.

I’m proud to display it front and center at our #IN06 offices. https://t.co/MPOeWbAfBa

assessment.summary: The captured X archive has 3 Kurdistan Lens tweet hits for Jefferson Shreve. The social-media posture reads as constructive or diplomatic signal because the strongest matched frame is support, partnership, or official-contact language. Top matched terms: Oil / Energy.
assessment.whatItWasLike: When this account mentioned the Kurdish file, the language most often clustered around support, partnership, or official-contact language. I count 0 direct Kurdistan/Kurdish-region term hits, 1 security-frame hit, 1 supportive or diplomatic hit, 0 humanitarian/minority hits, and 3 energy/economic hits in the exported profile window. The latest matched tweet is dated 2026-07-10T00:38:38Z and should be opened before treating this as a final stance.
